how we live so we need to do everything we can to reduce reduce perceived risk now some way that we can do it is with things like testimonial and case studies we can basically show social proof that channel so some of the psychological hacks or triggers that I want to bring to your attention are the mere exposure effect this just essentially says that the more times that we see something the more that we like it we like things that we see a lot we feel more comfortable with them this sort of stems back to our caveman days if we saw something roaming around the cave and it didn't try to eat us enough we we assumed it was friendly same thing goes with our ads this is why we want to be consistent with our copy with our branding with our messaging with retargeting we can uh sort of introduce into the puzzle later showing up again and again there's a old rule called the rule of seven in marketing where we want to make at least seven touch points with someone in order to get them to take action well this is what that's playing on is the mere exposure effect the next one is the reciprocity effect the reciprocity effect is essentially we're far more likely to want to return the favor if someone's done something nice for us in the first place there a great book by Robert chalini called influence talks about this as well as most other very important social triggers as well that you'll see pretty much everywhere around you uh but this one's very common this is the reason that um servers at restaurants will give out mints at the end of the meal or chocolates or something like that it significantly boosts tips as well in fact they' did studies where the servers gave a few extra chocolates or few extra tips a few extra mints and it increased the tips quite dramatically so regardless inevitably do make that purchase decision it's you that they think of but there's another benefit as well and this one is a little more psychological in nature and it's called the mere exposure effect and it is perhaps one of my all-time favorite psychological principles how this one works is we as human beings naturally like and grow fond of and appreciate things that we see more often simply because we see them more often essentially the more times that we're exposed to something the more that we like it it's why we're kind of distrustful of new or novel or um new people when they come into our lives whereas like the same person that we see all the time we we know them that's just it's just Bob just Bob being Bob anyway probably stems back from our caveman days when if we saw right little buddy and how a simple squirrel maze reminded me of a powerful lesson about marketing the other day my 9-year-old son was watching a video in class was the classic Mark Rober squirrel maze video If you haven't seen it it's an entertaining experiment where Mark Rober Builds an elaborate maze to outsmart squirrels trying to steal his bird seed naturally my son was hooked and inspired he rushed home with this grand plan to build a squirrel Maze of his very own in our backyard he'd drawn up Blueprints and collected a variety of nuts and ordered to test walnuts versus cashews and even began constructing a mockup maze with materials that he could find around the house like Lego and random wood scraps and some random household items naturally I was excited for him his creativity and initiative were impressive and looked like he was having a blast but as a marketer couldn't help but feel a little bit concerned because there was one important question that he hadn't considered so after admiring his work had to ask him hey buddy this is awesome but have you ever seen any squirrels in our backyard it was an important question the kind of question that you need to ask before investing time and energy and resources into any project his answer no but it's an easy mistake to make cuz our backyard's full of all kinds of other Wildlife rabbits and deer and ducks and Eagles and even the occasional black bear and cougar sometimes wander through but not a single squirrel at that moment I realized that this was more than just a nice father-son moment it was a powerful analogy for how many business owners out there approach their marketing you see so many business owners make the exact same mistake that my son did they get excited about a shiny new marketing tactic they've seen somewhere maybe something their competition is doing or a trending strategy on social media it seems like a great idea so they dive head first into it without really thinking it through maybe it's jumping on Tik Tok because everyone says it's the next big thing or maybe it's running Facebook ads because your competitor is doing it or maybe it's starting a blog because some marketing Guru said that it's a must have for SEO but here's the catch they never stop to ask themselves the critical question are my customers even there just like my son building a maze for squirrels that don't exist many businesses invest heavily in marketing strategies that don't actually reach their target audience put put their time and money and energy into platforms and tactics that aren't aligned with where their ideal customers are spending their Time online sadly the cost of chasing the wrong audience or chasing the Right audience but in the wrong location it's huge not only can It drain your
